2026-04-16T23:14:25.0101344Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T23:14:25.0101774Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T23:14:25.0102135Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T23:14:25.0102439Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T23:14:25.0102675Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-16T23:17:35.4784422Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T23:15:32.36804941Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58242,\"title\":\"[ResourceGraph] Docs: Fill in empty sections and add key concepts for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58068) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nA new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` library was added (1.0.0-beta.1) providing declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Resource Graph shared queries. The library exposes `ResourceGraphQuery` and a `ResultKind` enum. A working snippet for creating a shared query is present in both the README and test file.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication is handled in provisioning libraries (typically delegated to `azd` / Azure CLI; the library itself does not authenticate)\\n- **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to Azure Resource Graph documentation, quickstarts, or related Azure.Provisioning.* libraries\\n- **`Key concepts` section** doesn\\u0026#39;t document the `FromExisting` static method pattern, which is a core concept for all Azure.Provisioning.* libraries (allows referencing already-provisioned resources by Bicep identifier)\\n- **`ResultKind` enum** (read-only output property on `ResourceGraphQuery`) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md`\\n\\n**1a. Fill in `Authenticate the Client` section** (after line 19, before `## Key concepts`):\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nNo authentication is required to define your infrastructure using this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` generates Bicep templates that are deployed via the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) Authentication to Azure happens at deployment time through `azd` or the Azure CLI, not through this library.\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand `Key concepts` section** — add the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation. Replace:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\\n\\n### ResourceGraphQuery\\n\\n`ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Key writable properties include:\\n\\n| Property | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `Query` | The KQL query string to execute against Azure Resource Graph |\\n| `Description` | Human-readable description of the query |\\n| `ETag` | Optimistic concurrency token; omit to always overwrite |\\n| `Tags` | Freeform resource tags |\\n\\nThe following properties are read-only output values set by Azure after deployment:\\n\\n| Property | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `ResultKind` | Enum indicating the type of graph query result (`Basic` or other variants) |\\n| `ModifiedOn` | UTC timestamp of the last modification |\\n| `Id` | Full Azure resource identifier |\\n\\n### Referencing existing resources\\n\\nUse the static `FromExisting` method to reference a Resource Graph shared query that has already been provisioned, rather than creating a new one:\\n\\n```\\nResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\\n```\\n\\n\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Fill in `Next steps` section** — replace the empty section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Resource Graph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore [Azure Resource Graph query samples]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Get started with [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure\\n- Browse related [`Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries]((www.nuget.org/redacted) for other Azure services\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**2a.** Add a `FromExisting` snippet to the test file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`.\\n\\nAdd a new test method to `BasicResourceGraphTests`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic void ReferenceExistingResourceGraphQuery()\\n{\\n    Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n    #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\\n    ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\\n    #endregion\\n\\n    infra.Add(existingQuery);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**2b.** Add the snippet placeholder in the README under the `### Referencing existing resources` subsection (see Step 1b above):\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` currently has all required sections, but two are empty:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---------|--------|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1a |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — expand with Step 1b |\\n| Examples | ✅ Has snippet |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete |\\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1c |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Complete |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 2026-04-16T23:17:35.4844942Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T23:15:32.567598294Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58242,\"title\":\"[ResourceGraph] Docs: Fill in empty sections and add key concepts for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58068) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nA new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` library was added (1.0.0-beta.1) providing declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Resource Graph shared queries. The library exposes `ResourceGraphQuery` and a `ResultKind` enum. A working snippet for creating a shared query is present in both the README and test file.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication is handled in provisioning libraries (typically delegated to `azd` / Azure CLI; the library itself does not authenticate)\\n- **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to Azure Resource Graph documentation, quickstarts, or related Azure.Provisioning.* libraries\\n- **`Key concepts` section** doesn\\u0026#39;t document the `FromExisting` static method pattern, which is a core concept for all Azure.Provisioning.* libraries (allows referencing already-provisioned resources by Bicep identifier)\\n- **`ResultKind` enum** (read-only output property on `ResourceGraphQuery`) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md`\\n\\n**1a. Fill in `Authenticate the Client` section** (after line 19, before `## Key concepts`):\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nNo authentication is required to define your infrastructure using this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` generates Bicep templates that are deployed via the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) Authentication to Azure happens at deployment time through `azd` or the Azure CLI, not through this library.\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand `Key concepts` section** — add the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation. Replace:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\\n\\n### ResourceGraphQuery\\n\\n`ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Key writable properties include:\\n\\n| Property | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `Query` | The KQL query string to execute against Azure Resource Graph |\\n| `Description` | Human-readable description of the query |\\n| `ETag` | Optimistic concurrency token; omit to always overwrite |\\n| `Tags` | Freeform resource tags |\\n\\nThe following properties are read-only output values set by Azure after deployment:\\n\\n| Property | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `ResultKind` | Enum indicating the type of graph query result (`Basic` or other variants) |\\n| `ModifiedOn` | UTC timestamp of the last modification |\\n| `Id` | Full Azure resource identifier |\\n\\n### Referencing existing resources\\n\\nUse the static `FromExisting` method to reference a Resource Graph shared query that has already been provisioned, rather than creating a new one:\\n\\n```\\nResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\\n```\\n\\n\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Fill in `Next steps` section** — replace the empty section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Resource Graph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore [Azure Resource Graph query samples]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Get started with [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure\\n- Browse related [`Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries]((www.nuget.org/redacted) for other Azure services\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**2a.** Add a `FromExisting` snippet to the test file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`.\\n\\nAdd a new test method to `BasicResourceGraphTests`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic void ReferenceExistingResourceGraphQuery()\\n{\\n    Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n    #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\\n    ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\\n    #endregion\\n\\n    infra.Add(existingQuery);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**2b.** Add the snippet placeholder in the README under the `### Referencing existing resources` subsection (see Step 1b above):\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` currently has all required sections, but two are empty:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---------|--------|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1a |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — expand with Step 1b |\\n| Examples | ✅ Has snippet |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete |\\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1c |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Complete |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from test `#region` blocks into README placeholders; verify README diff shows the `FromExisting` snippet populated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for The library exposes `ResourceGraphQuery` and a `ResultKind` enum. A working snippet for creating a shared query is present in both the README and test file.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication is handled in provisioning libraries (typically delegated to `azd` / Azure CLI; the library itself does not authenticate)\\n- **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to Azure Resource Graph documentation, quickstarts, or related Azure.Provisioning.* libraries\\n- **`Key concepts` section** doesn't document the `FromExisting` static method pattern, which is a core concept for all Azure.Provisioning.* libraries (allows referencing already-provisioned resources by Bicep identifier)\\n- **`ResultKind` enum** (read-only output property on `ResourceGraphQuery`) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md`\\n\\n**1a. Fill in `Authenticate the Client` section** (after line 19, before `## Key concepts`):\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nNo authentication is required to define your infrastructure using this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` generates Bicep templates that are deployed via the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) Authentication to Azure happens at deployment time through `azd` or the Azure CLI, not through this library.\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand `Key concepts` section** — add the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation. Replace:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\\n\\n### ResourceGraphQuery\\n\\n`ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Key writable properties include:\\n\\n| Property | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `Query` | The KQL query string to execute against Azure Resource Graph |\\n| `Description` | Human-readable description of the query |\\n| `ETag` | Optimistic concurrency token; omit to always overwrite |\\n| `Tags` | Freeform resource tags |\\n\\nThe following properties are read-only output values set by Azure after deployment:\\n\\n| Property | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `ResultKind` | Enum indicating the type of graph query result (`Basic` or other variants) |\\n| `ModifiedOn` | UTC timestamp of the last modification |\\n| `Id` | Full Azure resource identifier |\\n\\n### Referencing existing resources\\n\\nUse the static `FromExisting` method to reference a Resource Graph shared query that has already been provisioned, rather than creating a new one:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\\nResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\\n```\\n\\n\\u003c/details_end\\u003e\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Fill in `Next steps` section** — replace the empty section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Resource Graph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore [Azure Resource Graph query samples]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Get started with [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure\\n- Browse related [`Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries]((www.nuget.org/redacted) for other Azure services\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**2a.** Add a `FromExisting` snippet to the test file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`.\\n\\nAdd a new test method to `BasicResourceGraphTests`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic void ReferenceExistingResourceGraphQuery()\\n{\\n    Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n    #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\\n    ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\\n    #endregion\\n\\n    infra.Add(existingQuery);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**2b.** Add the snippet placeholder in the README under the `### Referencing existing resources` subsection (see Step 1b above):\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` currently has all required sections, but two are empty:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---------|--------|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1a |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — expand with Step 1b |\\n| Examples | ✅ Has snippet |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete |\\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1c |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Complete |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from test `#region` blocks into README placeholders; verify README diff shows the `FromExisting` snippet populated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e
